reviews.review_description: I love this game but I thought this game could have (hopefully) a new update that was an extraction game mode that used wepons and upgrade you find through the massive map. And you if you extract succesfully you can use XP  gotten from killing enemies  to buy upgrades to use in future raids. I know this would be extremely challenging but thank you for reading my ramblings.

Really good game
For those who want to buy or get here is the base gameplay
PROGRESSION•
You start out with just a sword and dagger, but over time unlock weapons by collecting pieces one annoying thing about getting things though is how you have to reach the final boss to get some weapon upgrades.
GAMEPLAY•
This game is a simple, run of the mill, roguelite. One thing that differentiates it from others is the unique classes and multiplayer focus.But it is still Enjoyable in single player. But overall better white a group of friends.

I really enjoy this game, a long-time favourite!

When I got the notification about the supporter addon, I bought it instantly, without reading, because I want to support this dev. 🙌

(To be honest, I very rarely have chance to play, but this game became a core VR memory for me, when it was only in beta, and it feels so nice to see how much care this dev gives to this game)

Picked it up on Horizon+, and have been hooked. My only gripe is there are far too many children and almost no adults playing this, but that’s not on the game or Devs. Incredible dungeon diver though, coop when you find a good group is so satisfying. Can’t wait for more content!

reviews.review_title: The Wand & Tome Combo Feels Legendary!
reviews.review_description:
Ancient Dungeon VR absolutely nails that classic dungeon crawler feeling while still feeling fresh in VR. The pixel art style somehow works perfectly with the atmosphere, especially once you start getting deeper into the darker areas of the dungeon.
The combat is simple but ridiculously satisfying, and the Wand & Tome combo genuinely feels overpowered in the best way possible. Being able to blast enemies with magic while chaining attacks together makes you feel less like an adventurer and more like a full blown battle mage carving through the dungeon.
Every run feels different too thanks to the roguelike progression and randomised layouts, which keeps the game addictive even after multiple runs!
I love the game and for all the people sayin its too hard, thats just a skill issue. I prefer a game thats actually a challenge instead of a game you finish in a day.

I've tried a lot of VR games, and I really enjoy this one. It's easily my favorite. It's a great multi player game. The mechanics are fantastic. The dungons are fun and hard. The fighting is great. And the designers keep adding more to the game to make it better. It's simple and yet intense. With awesome colors. The only thing I don't like, is that I can't save after each boss. To complete an entire run, you have to make it through 5 dungons in a single run. Because of this, it forces the player to run through the dungon faster, so your headset doesn't die. And you have to commit to an entire two hour play. Also, I really hope they add more optional dungons with new monsters. I think new content would definitely help boost the game. Overall, two thumbs up. Great game.

I love this game I love dungeon crawling rougelikes and actually getting to be inside of one in vr is a dream come true (my only complaint is the movement options where a bit bad until monkey mode was added for context I have really had stick drift monkey mode made it so I could jot only move around easily but also actually play with out being forced into enemies) it’s amazing.

Its so good that im yanking. 

——-
Okay genuine review though, this game is actually really good. 

Game play is good, no problems 

I love the style of the game. Yummy art style >:p

No serious bugs- which is always a plus, especially with quest stand alone games. 

Also this years April fools update was peak. I hunted down the pots and mugged them as they begged for mercy.

They should make something for supporters like weapon things or something like that but nothing to make the game easier because that would make the game less fun but other then that the game is a 5 star the people are nice and fun and the game is very fun and its not to hard to learn

Very fun either by your self or with friends. I think the game is very interactive and full of many more possibilities in the future. I would however ask for maybe more weapons and some more maps. But all else is really good. Thank you devs for making a fun game!😁

The Devs prolly hate the comparison, but this is like a marriage between  Minecraft and Zelda.  Graphics line Minecraft, but gameplay like Zelda. You start with basic weapons, sword and throwing knife. Game originally seems difficult and hard to maneuver. Much like any good game tho, the learning curve is fast and fun. So far, am loving this game .   Easy 5 outta 5

reviews.review_title: Best VR game ever but bugs = unplayable
reviews.review_description:
You’re adding so much crap it’s breaking your game. Unreliable saves that destroy an entire run. Movement lags during fights. Merchant spawns with unobtainable invisible items.

Can’t load my saved dungeon run, just get black screen of death no matter how long I wait. Pretty unacceptable in a roguelike 

Get 5 stars again when this is fixed

developer_response.body:
Hey Mer1d1an!

Sorry that you're experiencing some bugs. That should not be happening, could you come over to our Discord for a chat so we can investigate? Most of our users are not experiencing these problems so we can most likely fix this for you with some dedicated support!

Our discord is: https://discord.gg/advr

Thank you for the feedback.

reviews.review_title: Great game there should be more!
reviews.review_description: The first time I played this game I thought it was the best game I’ve gotten for quest so far. After while I never got bored, but after adding mods I think the developers should add more weapons to the base game. Another thing they should add is shields, it would a different aspect to the game. It would make battles easier for people like me who have a hard time with some of the combat. The last thing the devs should add is a left, right, and back dodging mechanics. Sometimes in battles parrying with the sword isn’t enough. Overall I though the game was amazing and I hope the suggestions help.

Add a sword🗡️ and shield🛡️ into the game please that’s able to block except the spikes that you find from the mineshaft ￼boss. but make it so the shield breaks after too many shielded ￼attacks and you have to wait 10 seconds for you to get it back ￼👍👍

great game, artstyle is great, gameplay loop is fun.
the game IS difficult, you'll likely lose the first like 10-30 runs.
recent updates have brought a lot of replayability to the game, despite it already having a lot of it.
has good modding support, PCVR-quest crossplay, and pretty consistent updates.

i'm being serious, do not listen to bad reviews. most people who review the game badly are either bad at it or simply don't understand it. the game IS balanced well, it does feel good in VR, and it IS a roguelite, believe it or not, you lose progress when you die, that's literally the point of the game.

also, yes, the game is a voxel game, it has pixel art and the 3d world is made of cubes. it's not minecraft.

the game is also in early access, it has bugs. there are also bugs after an update, things aren't well balanced after updates decently often, it's not surprising, and the devs actually care about it, so they do fix things.

I've had this game from the very early stages and they have make a TON of changes over time (all of them fantastic).  This game is a blast.  I play with my daughter on a regular basis and the changes have us playing it more now than we ever have before.  It does a great job of capturing that 'dungeon crawler' feel.  Don't let the visual style make you think this is a kids game, there is a lot combat and exploration in this game.  Fantastic themes to levels and really feels immersive.

From the recently added weapons, to the new progression system, to the new weapon modification systems, it just keeps getting better and better.  I highly recommend this if you have a friend or two to play with, but the game is even fun solo.

Really looking forward to whatever else they have cooked up.  Easily becoming our most played VR game in our vast collection of titles.

This game is amazing if you love roguelikes and your VR. It has lots of cool mechanics and items to randomize gameplay. My only complaint is the massive difficulty spike from levels 1 to 2. I've never made it past 3, but this game provides a nice challenge.
edit: made this a while ago and I guess I needed to get good. or something

reviews.review_title: Genuinely the most addictive game for me
reviews.review_description:
I’ve played Ancient Dungeons on and off ever since I got a Quest 2 for Christmas when gorilla tag was huge. I found out about the demo and eventually got around to playing the full version. As of today, I’ve done just about everything you can do in the game. I’ve gotten every relic (minus 1), every memory, every augment, every insight upgrade, every journal page. I’d go into week long phases where it’s just all I play in my free time. I love it. 

I have no idea how commonly people would agree with me on this, but I don’t feel like the game gets very repetitive at all in the general gameplay loop. There’s so many different ways you can play, so many different ways delves could go. Never once did I feel like I was playing the same thing twice. Despite that, there is one thing I wish I could see more of in the game that would make the game way more refreshing and engaging, and that’s combat mechanics. I think the new weapons really helped to make that difference in gameplay, and I love getting to use the new weapons every time new ones come out. The weapons start to feel less fun though once you use them against each type of enemy already.

Monsters are the second side to the combat in the game, so generally I wonder why the game hasn’t done more to enhance the monsters like they do with the weapons? Late game, the bosses start to feel less like bosses and more just like more interesting regular monsters. I think adding more mechanics and gimmicks to enemies could make a huge difference in the game. For example, when the noxious sewers and the luminous depths were added, the enemies were mostly repeats of the alternate floor’s enemies. The saving grace was the unique generation of the floors, and the flies, hives, and those dashing grubs. The luminous depths probably could use a revamp. Anyways, the thing that made it feel refreshing was the new enemies with their new gimmicks. An earlier addition to the game I especially loved was the possessed armor. The blocking mechanic is super unique. And I really wonder why the content like the possessed vine and the overgrown zombie champion were removed.

What I think you should likely do with all that is try to add more complex enemy types, that way the fights aren’t just the same flow with different arrangements every time. Also as a side note, for the sake of challenging players, I’d make alternative floors chosen randomly instead of giving a player the choice between the two. It’s easier to choose the floor that’s less difficult, which reduces variety in the game.

Other than that, I still can’t stress enough though just how much I enjoy playing Ancient Dungeons just how it is though. That’s just my personal recommendation for the game.

Its a great game, the random dungeons are incredible and so is discovering new things replaying the game it actually has interesting lore to read from and a fun class system you can change from time to time to keep it different, plus being able to add perks to your equipment. This is one of the very few games I still play in vr. I've always enjoyed rougelikes and dungeon crawlers, and this game is a realization of exactly what i'd want in a vr game like that! Great replayability, charming visuals, fun and immersive movement (I can't NOT mention monkey movement), a nice OST, and quite a few ways to customize your experience to your own liking! 10/10, would recommend.
